title: Highlights: Two from Merstone wines in Bedfordshire, UK
summary: Merstone Wines Hungerhill Vineyard Chardonnay Voltis 2024 Bedfordhshire, England11% alcohol. Hand picked in October, then fermented and aged in Burgundy
content:
Merstone Wines Hungerhill Vineyard Chardonnay Voltis 2024 Bedfordhshire, England
11% alcohol. Hand picked in October, then fermented and aged in Burgundy barrels. Unfiltered and unfined. This has a hint of leafy elderflower on the nose, but also some ripe citrus fruit. The palate is bright and textured with a bit of spicy depth from the oak, showing vivid citrus fruit, a touch of green apple and pear, and then some nice acidity on the finish, together with some saline hints. It’s a really good wine, offering character and pleasure. 90/100

Merstone Wines Hungerhill Vineyard Pet-Nat 2025 Bedfordhshire, England
Dornfelder and Bacchus are the grapes here. It gushes a bit on opening (this is undisgorged), with maybe half a glass leaving, so take care on opening. It’s cloudy with some yeasty cherry and strawberry aromas. In the mouth this is bright and lively with some green hints, a touch of elderflower, some peach and also some cranberry. It’s really tasty and engaging, finishing with brisk acidity. There’s a lovely savoury, yeasty, bready undercurrent to the fruit here. 90/100

title: RPM, a music-inspired low-intervention winery from Portugal
summary: Website: https://www.vinrpm.com/en/ The basis of this music-inspired project is a family farm, which has been owned since the 1930s by
content:
Website: https://www.vinrpm.com/en/

The basis of this music-inspired project is a family farm, which has been owned since the 1930s by the Roseira family.

The farm is Quinta da Livração, and it’s in the transition zone between Vinho Verde and the Douro, in a small valley. There are 4 hectares of vines here.

In 2010 José Roseira took over the vineyard, and making these wines is a new project in collaboration with Ricardo Moreira and Daniel Pires.

Winemaking is natural. I tasted these wines at the 2026 Simplesmente Vinho festival.

RPM Analogue 2024 Portugal
Arinto, Avesso, and Fernão Pires, foot trodden in granite lagares and finishes fermenting in old concrete tanks after 3 days on skins. Complex, savoury and spicy. Slight mouse.

RPM EQ 2024 Portugal
Arinto and Loureiro grapes macerate separately for three days and finish fermenting together in a concrete tank. Vivid textured, juicy, finely spiced, buscuitty finish. 89/100

RPM Overdub 2024 Portugal
Field blend from old vines. 8 days in lagar then concrete. Savoury spicy edge to the rich citrus and peach fruit, with nice structure. 93/100

RPM Reverb 2023 Portugal
Avesso on skins for 8 month plus red wine stems. Powerful but elegant lovely spicy detail peach and pear. So layered. 94/100

RPM Overdub Tinto 2023 Portugal
Mostly Castelão. Lively acidity with some lift. Structured and dense with grainy detail. Vivid intense style. 90/100

title: Highlights: Nyetimber Rosé NV England
summary: This is the latest from English sparkling wine producer Nyetimber. They now have 11 vineyards (totalling 425 hectares) in West
content:
This is the latest from English sparkling wine producer Nyetimber. They now have 11 vineyards (totalling 425 hectares) in West Sussex, Hampshire, and Kent, and what they do so well is top quality at scale, which means you can find the wines widely distributed. This is a limited edition summer rosé: the wine is the same but it comes in flashy packaging. I tasted this today and was really impressed. Wine on camera coming tomorrow.

Nyetimber Rosé NV England
Beautiful balance here with sweet berry and cherry fruits as well as crisp citrus and a bit of spicy grip on the finish. It’s overtly fruity but grown up, with plenty of complexity to hold the attention. Quite serious. 93/100

title: Esterházy, a historical estate reborn, making lovely wines from Austria’s Leithaberg (Burgenland) region
summary: Website: https://esterhazywein.at/en/ Esterházy is a historic estate, located in the Leithaberg subregion of Burgenland. The Esterházy castle, owned by the
content:
Website: https://esterhazywein.at/en/

Esterházy is a historic estate, located in the Leithaberg subregion of Burgenland. The Esterházy castle, owned by the royals when this was part of Hungary, and still in royal hands, is quite an imposing building and hosts lots of musical events. It’s also a tourist destination. Famously, the royal family sponsored the composer Joseph Hadyn, who spent most of his working life (30 years) working for them and living on the estate.

Esterházy has always made wine, but it’s only recently that they’ve upped their game significantly, with new investment, including a re-badging of the label. They are a big player for the region, with 65 hectares or organically farmed vineyards spread over 21 plots. Everything has been organically certified from the 2023 vintage.

You can tell whether the wines are regional, village, or single vineyard by how many of the three design items on the label are in gold. It’s all three for the single-vineyard wines.

Esterházy Sekt Rosé Reserve 2023 Burgenland, Austria
This is Blaufränkisch, pressed, and then some red wine added as part of the dosage for colouring. Bright, fresh and fruity after 18 months on lees (dosage c. 5 g/l). Juicy and fresh with nice acidity. 90/100

Esterházy Sekt NV Burgenland, Austria
Blaufränkisch, with the base wine 2024. Juicy, fresh, linear and pure with a hint of cherry. Quite stylish and linear. 90/100

Esterházy Ried Herrschafsbreite Grosse Reserve Blanc de Noir 2019 Burgenland, Austria
This fizz is a Brut Nature, and it was fermented in stainless steel and then spent 36 months on lees. Bright, fresh and linear with a nice lemony drive. Fresh and pure with cherries and lime. 92/100

Esterházy Furmint Sankt Margarethen 2024 Burgenland, Austria
A village level wine, from a mix of schist and limestone soils. Matured in 1000 litre Stockinger barrels made from forests behind the village. Textured and fine with nice sweet pear and peach fruit. Balanced with a lovely sheen to it. 93/100

Esterházy Grüner Veltliner Grosshöflein 2024 Burgenland, Austria
Nice supple wine showing good concentration of lovely pear and white peach fruit with a hint of white pepper. Good presence and depth here. 93/100

Esterházy Pinot Blanc Grosshöflein 2024 Burgenland, Austria
Mainly limestone soils. Fine and expressive, pure and linear with a good acid line. Very stylish with finesse. 94/100

Esterházy Ried Oberer Wald Pinot Blanc 2023 Burgenland, Austria
Pure limestone soil. Supple with a hint of cabbage, and nice pear and citrus fruit. Some depth here with nice acidity. 94/100

Esterházy Chardonnay Sankt Margarethen  2024 Burgenland, Austria
Three different vineyards, a mixture of limestone and schist. Raised in 1500 litre and 500 litre oak, 10% new. Nice weight to the pear and peach fruit, with good acidity. This has real texture and depth, and it’s stylish. 93/100

Esterházy Ried Lamer Chardonnay 2023 Burgenland, Austria
From schist, two 500 litre barrels. Lovely weight of pear and peach fruit. Fine and expressive with some tension and detail. 94/100

Esterházy Blaufrankisch Estate 2023 Burgenland, Austria
Supple, open, juicy and bright. Nice cherry and plum fruit with some raspberry fruit. Very pure with a hint of grip on the finish. A lovely wine. 93/100

Esterházy Pinot Noir Grosshöflein 2023 Burgenland, Austria
Bright and vivid with juicy cherry fruit and nice acidity, as well as some structure. Fresh and tart with purity, finishing tightwound. 92/100

Esterházy Blaufränkisch Sankt Georgen 2023 Burgenland, Austria
A supple, elegant style with vivid cherry and plum fruit, as well as nice grainy structure and a hint of gravel. Classic with nice weight. 93/100

Esterházy Ried Schildten Blaufränkisch 2023 Burgenland, Austria
This is a monopole vineyard that they bought in 1658. South-facing sloped vineyard that’s quite warm, with 35 year old vines. Schist and limestone. Spends 3 weeks on skins then aged in two 500 litre barrels. Vivid, grainy and grippy with lovely weight and focus. Structured and delicious. 94/100

Esterházy Project Wine 7 2023 Burgenland, Austria
This is Blaufränkisch, carbonic maceration in stainless steel. Supple with some herbal hints and nice sweet cherry and plum fruit, as well as some ripe strawberry. Open, expressive, delicious. 93/100

Esterházy Project Wine 25 2024 Burgenland, Austria
This is a Pet Nat rosé, made from Blaufränkisch, and disgorged. Juicy and lively, with bright cherry and lemon fruit. Clean and appealing and really well made. 90/100

Esterházy Project Wine 16 2023 Burgenland, Austria
This is Grüner Veltliner fermented and raised in a granite cask! 550 bottles made. Juicy and bright with pure fruit. Linear with good acidity, showing nice stony detail. Very stylish. 91/100

Esterházy Project Wine 20 CHE 2023 Burgenland, Austria
This is Chenin Blanc. Lovely apple and lemon fruit. Juicy and expressive with nice brightness and also some depth. 92/100

title: Netzl and Christina, two generations from the same winery in Austria’s Carnuntum region
summary: Website: https://www.netzl.com/en Many Austrian wineries are family owned, and the change of generations is a way that some use to
content:
Website: https://www.netzl.com/en

Many Austrian wineries are family owned, and the change of generations is a way that some use to reinvent themselves. That’s happening at Netzl, where Christina Netzl is making a new range of wines that exists alongside the traditional wines of her father Franz (they are pictured above). Both ranges are excellent, and it’s great to see this sort of reinvention.

The Netzl vineyard, near the village of Göttlesbrun, is 30 hectares, all farmed organically, with a split of 60:40 red to white. They focus on the hillside vineyard for their single-vineyard wines. These sites are surrounded by trees, which cools things down nicely. Soils are gravelly.

I tasted these wines with Christina at Vievinum in Vienna.

Netzl Chardonnay 2025 Carnuntum, Austria
This is a regional wine, and spends 5 months in stainless steel on full lees. Lively, bright fruit here with supple lemony notes and good acidity. 89/100

Netzel Göttlesbrunn 2024 Carnuntum, Austria
This is a blend of Chardonnay and Grüner Veltliner, with the Chardonnay spending a year in amphora (750 litre Tava) on full lees. Lovely textured and depth with pure citrus and pear fruit with some white peach richness. Fine grained. 92/100

Netzl Ried Altenberg Weissburgunder 2024 Carnuntum, Austria
Made in a mix of 500 litre tonneaux and amphorae. Complex and layered with fresh fruit, rounded texture and a slight salinity. Really good. 94/100

Netzl Ried Altenberg Chardonnay 2024 Carnuntum, Austria
Made in a mix of oak and amphorae. Lovely tension here and a bit of saltiness. Pure, fine and expressive. Delicate but with tension. 93/100

Netzl Rosanna Rosé 2025 Carnuntum, Austria
Zweigelt, half direct press, half saignée. Fresh, bright, pure, linear, quite transparent, easy. 88/100

Christina Grüner Veltliner 2024 Carnuntum, Austria
The only addition is 20 mg SO2 before bottling, and this wine is bottled with fine lees. Supple and textural with nice weight and a rounded palate. Notes of white pepper support the pear and peach fruit. 92/100

Christina Rosé 2025 Carnuntum, Austria
Zweigelt, mostly saignée. Juicy with nice weight. Fruity and expressive with red cherries and pear. Stylish and textural. 91/100

Christina Orange 2025 Carnuntum, Austria
This is Chardonnay, Sauvignon Blanc and Welschriesling, skin fermented separately and spending 3 weeks on skins. No added sulfites. Lovely weight of pear and peach fruit with notes of almond and mint. Good balance with some structure. 93/100

Christina Gelber Muskateller 2025 Carnuntum, Austria
Skin fermented, five weeks on skins, aged in 500 litre vats. Floral and grapey with nice weight and good structure. Has some pear and table grape fruit. Grippy finish. 92/100

Christina Kuhlbar 2025 Carnuntum, Austria
This is Zweigelt, with a portion direct pressed, the rest a short time on skins. A lighter-style red that’s fresh and juicy with nice texture to the cherry and plum fruit, backed up with good acidity. Stylish. 93/100

Christina St Laurent 2021 Carnuntum, Austria
11% alcohol. Whole bunch, semi-carbonic. Supple and juicy with a green hint, and nice freshness and weight on the palate. Evolving nicely, and still tart and fresh. 92/100

Christina Zweigelt 2023 Carnuntum, Austria
This is short maceration then ageing in big oak. Juicy and tart with lively sour cherry and plum fruit, with some grippy structure. Nice definition: lively and crunchy. 91/100

Netzl Rubin Carnuntum 2023 Austria
Rubin Carnuntum is a regional style of Zweigelt. This is fresh with nice texture. Bright with good concentration, this is juicy and stylish. 93/100

Netzl Prellenkirchen Blaufränkisch 2022 Carnuntum, Austria
This is made from grapes they buy in from the Spitzerberg. Concentrated, chalky and mineral with nice density. Stylish showing freshness and depth, as well as some structure. 94/100

Netzl Göttlesbrun Edes Tal 2022 Carnuntum, Austria
Zweigelt, Merlot and Syrah. Fresh, supple and spicy with nice structure. Quite classic in style. Has a chalky edge to the vivid fruit, with nice density and weight. Stylish. 93/100

Netzl St Laurent 2023 Carnuntum, Austria
Destemmed, short maceration, then goes to 500 litre barrels. Lively and rounded with supple fruit. There’s a sweet core to the palate, with nice texture and balance. 93/100

Netzl Zweigelt Ried Haidacker 1er 2022 Carnuntum, Austria
Loess and gravel soils, 10% whole cluster. Very fine, fresh and expressive with nice tannic structure and a floral edge to the fruit. Some well judged oak. 93/100

Netzl Zweigelt Ried Haidacker 1er 2020 Carnuntum, Austria
Supple and bright, and linear, with nice juiciness and some structure, finishing with a sour cherry twist. Very stylish. 94/100

Netzl Ried Bärnreiser 1er Anna-Christina 2022 Carnuntum, Austria
50% Zweigelt, 30% Blaufränkisch, 20% Merlot. Bright and elegant with nice concentration, good tannins, and nice focus and grip. Linear, with real energy, and a touch of oak. Still tightwound. 93/100

title: Filippi: natural expressions of Soave, Italy
summary: Filippi are based in Soave, and the property has 20 hectares of forest as well as 14 hectares of vines.
content:
Filippi are based in Soave, and the property has 20 hectares of forest as well as 14 hectares of vines. The highest point of the vineyard is 400 m, and this is a transition point between basaltic and limestone soils. Filippo Filippi has been making wines naturally here for more than 20 years now, and they are full of interest.

Winemaking is all in stainless steel tanks with wild yeast ferments.

Filippi Vigdagòti NV IGT Verona, Italy
11.5% alcohol. One litre bottle. Garganega. Textured and mineral with supple citrus, pear and green apple fruit, with nice spicy framing. Nutritious and delicious. 91/100

Filippi Castelcerino 2024 Soave Colli Scaligeri, Italy
11% alcohol. This is from volcanic soils. Supple, finely textured and quite mineral, with notes of lemons, green apple and a hint of melon. Nice stony mineral undercurrent to the sweet, lively fruit. 93/100

Filippi Monteseroni Garganega 2023 IGT Verona, Italy
Garganega from limestone. Sometimes this is bottled as Soave but it can fail the taste test at the commission. This is fine-grained, textural and grainy with nice acidity. So fine grained with a stony, mineral edge, showing good acidity but also some depth. Lovely. 94/100

Filippi Turbiani Trebbiano di Soave IGT Verona, Italy
This is from a massal selection of 30 year old vines in the forest, on limestone soils. Fine and expressive with a lovely mineral edge and real delicacy. Fine and stony with beautiful poise and elegance. 94/100

title: Three nice rosés: Boutari, Delaunay, Foncalieu
summary: It’s rosé season, and here are three attractive, well priced examples, including a nice one from Greece. Boutari Dianthos Xinomavro
content:
It’s rosé season, and here are three attractive, well priced examples, including a nice one from Greece.

Boutari Dianthos Xinomavro Rosé 2025 PGI Imathia, Naoussa, Greece
12.5% alcohol. This is fresh and lively with some mandarin and citrus notes, as well as red cherry and cranberry. It’s fruity and delicious, but there’s a brightness and verticality to it that makes it quite gastronomic. Very stylish and well priced, too. 90/100 (£10 Tesco)

Joël Delaunay Les Cabotines Rosé 2025 Touraine, Loire, France
12.5% alcohol. Thierry Delaunay is a well respected Touraine vigneron, and this wine is made from Pineau D’Aunis and Gamay. Pale in colour, it’s stony and bright with raspberry and cranberry fruit, as well as some juiciness. This has a delicious tartness with subtle sappy hints, and lots of gastronomic potential. 91/100

Foncalieu Le Versant Grenache Rosé 2025 IGP Pays d’Oc, France
13% alcohol. Beautifully presented with a painted label design from artist Barthélémy Lanzini. This is a deliciously expressive rose with a sweet core of cherries and berries, with a fine spicy edge. There’s texture and richness here, showing deliciousness and drinkability, but also some nice spicy detail and an innate fruit sweetness. 90/100

title: Videos: Robots in the vineyard, new technology applied
summary: Viticulture is expensive, and a lot of the cost is paying people to do quite boring, repetitive work. Of course,
content:
Viticulture is expensive, and a lot of the cost is paying people to do quite boring, repetitive work. Of course, it’s great to give people jobs, but in many regions worldwide it can be a challenge finding people, especially for seasonal tasks that require a lot of people. Here I’ve collated some YouTube videos of vineyard robotics, beginning with one of my own.

This is a film I shot of the Vitibot Bakus in operation in Burgundy – it’s now quite widely used in France

This is a robot called Frasky in operation in Italy

This is a robot called Burro Grande that can be used to mow

And here are some robots in action at Pernod Ricard’s Brancott Estate in New Zealand

Here’s a trial of a robot that can actually pick grapes, being used in the UK

And here’s another grape-picking robot

Another robot, called Traxx. This, like Bakus, is suited to low canopies and close-planted vineyards with its over-row design.

Vinbot is used to estimate yield – an important job

These are Thorvald robots used to deliver UV-C to defeat powdery mildew. They need to be used in the dark.

Same technology: UV-C robots

And here’s a trial of a legged robot! Can do rocky vineyards.

title: Highlights: two from low intervention English producer Matt Gregory
summary: Website: https://mattgregorywines.co.uk/ Matt Gregory makes wines from a single 3 hectare vineyard in Leicestershire (UK), a county not normally associated
content:
Website: https://mattgregorywines.co.uk/

Matt Gregory makes wines from a single 3 hectare vineyard in Leicestershire (UK), a county not normally associated with winegrowing. The wines are naturally made, in small quantities, and they are fabulous.

Matt Gregory Wines Little Sister NV England
9% alcohol. This is a blend of two vintages.From 2023, 20% Pinot Noir, 14% Pinot Gris and from 2024, 47% Bacchus, 10% Regent, 9% Seyval Blanc. Direct pressed Bacchus from 2024 was aged in stainless steel and then blended with co-fermented Regent and Seyval after the Winter. A single barrel of Pinot Noir and Pinot Gris from the 2023 vintage was also then blended in. The only addition was a small sulfur dioxide addition before bottling in August 2025. This is pale in colour, and light and expressive on the nose with green-tinged red cherries, cranberries and elderflower. The palate is fresh and juicy with good acidity and some lovely lemon, mandarin and cherry notes, with a touch of redcurrant. It’s like a clarete in style, with more colour and structure than a rose, but it behaves quite like a white wine gastronomically, with keen acidity. The low alcohol really brings out the tart qualities of the fruit really nicely. There’s enough complexity to hold the interest, and it’s a beautifully expressive, personality-full wine. 93/100 (£27.50)

Matt Gregory Wines Two Sisters 2023 England
10% alcohol. 60/40 Pinot Noir/Pinot Gris. Destemmed and co-fermented, then pressed to stainless steel and a single used barrique from Barolo (25% of the blend). Only addition was minor sulfite add before bottling. Quite pale in colour, this has a sappy nose with green leafy notes and some cherry and redcurrant. The palate shows some structure and a hint of savoury spiciness, as well as tart red fruits, but also a bit of ripe pear richness, and some wild strawberry. There’s a lot going on here: a lighter-style red with real personality and character. 93/100 (£37.50)

title: Highlights: Champagne Gosset Cuvée Dosage Zéro
summary: Website: https://www.champagne-gosset.com/en/our-champagne-wines/cuvee-zero-dosage/ This is a new cuvée from Gosset. The wine is based on the 2013 vintage and was bottled
content:
Website: https://www.champagne-gosset.com/en/our-champagne-wines/cuvee-zero-dosage/

This is a new cuvée from Gosset. The wine is based on the 2013 vintage and was bottled in 2015, then disgorged 10 years later. The base was given more than a year on lees to add richness and complexity. This is quite beautiful.

Champagne Gosset Cuvée Dosage Zéro NV France
12% alcohol. Chardonnay 62%, Pinot Noir 38%, from a range of villages including Ambonnay, Avize, Bouzy, Cumières, Cramant, Villers-Marmery and Rilly la Montagne. Taut and expressive with lovely acidity supporting the rich, crystalline citrus fruit, together with a hint of apple and a long, tangy, slightly saline finish. This is vinous and delicious, and so fresh and gastronomic. A brilliant wine. 95/100
